585	async function getConversation(
586	  store: ConversationStore,
587	  bus: HookBus,
588	  ctx: AgentContext,
589	  input: GetInput,
590	): Promise<GetOutput> {
591	  const conv = await store.getByIdNotDeleted(input.conversationId);
592	  if (conv === null || conv.userId !== input.userId) {
593	    throw new PluginError({
594	      code: 'not-found',
595	      plugin: PLUGIN_NAME,
596	      hookName: 'conversations:get',
597	      message: `conversation '${input.conversationId}' not found`,
598	    });
599	  }
600	  await assertAgentReachable(
601	    bus,
602	    ctx,
603	    conv.agentId,
604	    input.userId,
605	    'conversations:get',
606	  );
607	  // Phase D (2026-05-02): the transcript lives in the runner's native
608	  // jsonl file inside the workspace, NOT in conversation_turns. We
609	  // skip the workspace round-trip entirely when runnerSessionId is
610	  // null (pre-Phase-C rows or fresh rows whose first turn hasn't
611	  // landed yet — see Q3=(a)). The wire shape on
612	  // `conversations:get` is unchanged.
613	  const turns =
614	    conv.runnerSessionId === null
615	      ? []
616	      : await readTranscriptFromWorkspace(bus, ctx, conv.runnerSessionId);
617	  return { conversation: conv, turns };
618	}

289	      // ----- session:get-config -----
290	      //
291	      // The runner calls this at boot via IPC. The IPC server's authenticate()
292	      // resolved the runner's bearer token to a sessionId and stamped that
293	      // onto ctx.sessionId — we read it here. There is intentionally NO
294	      // sessionId in the input; closing that door means a runner can't
295	      // ask for someone else's config.
296	      bus.registerService<SessionGetConfigInput, SessionGetConfigOutput>(
297	        'session:get-config',
298	        PLUGIN_NAME,
299	        async (ctx, _input) => {
300	          const hookName = 'session:get-config';
301	          requireString(ctx.sessionId, 'ctx.sessionId', hookName);
302	          const record = store.get(ctx.sessionId);
303	          if (record === null || record.terminated) {
304	            throw new PluginError({
305	              code: UNKNOWN_SESSION,
306	              plugin: PLUGIN_NAME,
307	              hookName,
308	              message: `session '${ctx.sessionId}' does not exist or has been terminated`,
309	            });
310	          }
311	          if (
312	            record.userId === null ||
313	            record.agentId === null ||
314	            record.agentConfig === null
315	          ) {
316	            // Pre-9.5 session OR a session minted by a non-orchestrator
317	            // path. Either way, there's no agent config to hand back —
318	            // refuse explicitly. The runner treats this as a hard boot
319	            // error (it has no system prompt to use).
320	            throw new PluginError({
321	              code: 'owner-missing',
322	              plugin: PLUGIN_NAME,
323	              hookName,
324	              message: `session '${ctx.sessionId}' has no owner — minted before Week 9.5 or by a non-orchestrator caller`,
325	            });
326	          }
327	          return {
328	            userId: record.userId,
329	            agentId: record.agentId,
330	            agentConfig: record.agentConfig,
331	            conversationId: record.conversationId,
332	          };
333	        },
334	      );

1558	  // resume; only the first one is load-bearing for the bind. The handler
1559	  // sets a flag BEFORE awaiting the IPC so a re-entrant init can't
1560	  // double-fire.

1810	  // Phase C: SDK resume(sessionId) instead of replay-from-DB.
1811	  //
1812	  // When `conversation.fetch-history` returns a non-null `runnerSessionId`,
1813	  // the runner passes it as `options.resume` to `query()` and SKIPS the
1814	  // user-turn replay (the SDK rehydrates the conversation from its own
1815	  // on-disk transcript). When `runnerSessionId` is null, we fall back to
1816	  // the prior replay-from-DB path (Task 15).
1817	  //
1818	  // The trade-off the controller chose: when `runnerSessionId !== null`,
1819	  // the host's bus hook still reads turns from the DB even though the
1820	  // runner ignores them. That extra DB hit is cheap (one query at boot)
1821	  // and keeps the wire shape simple — a single response covers both the

1981	  // The SDK can't write its jsonl into /nonexistent, so the targeted fix

1984	  // jsonl lands inside the workspace where the turn-end

1986	  // process's own git operations still see HOME=/nonexistent because
1987	  // we don't override their env.

41	async function makeEnv(): Promise<Env> {
42	  const bus = new HookBus();

45	    plugins: [createSessionInmemoryPlugin()],

3	import type { HandlerErr } from './handlers/types.js';
4	
5	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
6	// Error mapping
7	//
8	// Thin helpers that translate host-side failure modes into the sanitized
9	// wire-shape the client sees. The invariant (I9 + "no info leak") is:
10	//
11	//   - The client receives { code: IpcErrorCode, message: <safe string> }.
12	//   - The caller-side logger receives the real error (hookName, plugin,
13	//     cause) at `error` level for debugging.
14	//
15	// Callers pass the logger's `ctx.logger.error` into `logInternalError()` so
16	// the leak-prevention is localized to these helpers — dispatcher and
17	// handlers can't accidentally spill a stack trace by returning the wrong
18	// shape. The wire-shape helpers below (`validationError`, `notFound`,
19	// `hookRejected`, `mapPluginError`, `internalError`) produce
20	// sanitized responses; `logInternalError` is the matching observability
21	// side that logs the real cause for debugging.
22	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
23	
24	/** Validation failure — bad JSON shape, missing fields, etc. */
25	export function validationError(message: string): HandlerErr {
26	  return { status: 400, body: { error: { code: 'VALIDATION', message } } };
27	}
28	
29	/** 404 for "unknown route / unknown host tool" — distinct from 400 VALIDATION
30	 *  (malformed request) since the client can legitimately ask about a tool the
31	 *  host doesn't expose. */
32	export function notFound(message: string): HandlerErr {
33	  return { status: 404, body: { error: { code: 'NOT_FOUND', message } } };
34	}
35	
36	/** A subscriber chain returned `reject`; the action is vetoed. */
37	export function hookRejected(reason: string): HandlerErr {
38	  return { status: 409, body: { error: { code: 'HOOK_REJECTED', message: reason } } };
39	}
40	
41	/**
42	 * Map a PluginError thrown inside a service hook call to a safe wire error.
43	 * Internal codes never leak to the client; only the bucketed HTTP status
44	 * and a fixed message do.
45	 *
46	 * `conflict` is mapped to 409 HOOK_REJECTED — used by
47	 * `conversations:store-runner-session` (Phase C) when an idempotent bind
48	 * sees a different runner session id already attached to the row.
49	 */
50	export function mapPluginError(err: PluginError): HandlerErr {
51	  switch (err.code) {
52	    case 'invalid-payload':
53	      return validationError('invalid payload');
54	    // Session backend codes (Week 9.5). Unknown / terminated sessions
55	    // map to 401 because the bearer token the runner is authenticating
56	    // with no longer resolves to a usable session — the runner should
57	    // exit, not retry. `owner-missing` (legacy pre-9.5 row reaching
58	    // session:get-config) is a configuration mismatch, not a transient
59	    // failure; map to 401 too so the runner fails fast.
60	    case 'unknown-session':
61	    case 'owner-missing':
62	      return {
63	        status: 401,
64	        body: { error: { code: 'SESSION_INVALID', message: 'session has no usable config' } },
65	      };
66	    // Task 15 (Week 10–12): conversations:fetch-history surfaces these
67	    // codes from its ACL gate (`not-found` for cross-tenant or unknown
68	    // rows, `forbidden` for `agents:resolve` denial). Channel-web maps
69	    // the SAME bus errors as 404 / 403 over HTTP; we mirror that here
70	    // so the runner sees the same outcome regardless of caller. We use
71	    // HOOK_REJECTED with HTTP 403 (the IPC error-code enum doesn't have
72	    // a FORBIDDEN constant — minting one would widen the wire surface
73	    // and isn't load-bearing for the runner, which only branches on
74	    // status to decide retry-vs-fatal).
75	    case 'not-found':
76	      return notFound('not found');
77	    case 'forbidden':
78	      return {
79	        status: 403,
80	        body: { error: { code: 'HOOK_REJECTED', message: 'forbidden' } },
81	      };
82	    // Phase C (runner-owned sessions). conversations:store-runner-session
83	    // throws `conflict` when the conversation row is already bound to a
84	    // different runnerSessionId. The runner should treat this as fatal
85	    // (the binding it observed locally diverges from what the host has
86	    // recorded for an earlier turn) — 409 maps to a non-retryable error
87	    // on the client side.
88	    case 'conflict':
89	      return hookRejected('conflict');
90	    case 'no-service':
91	    case 'duplicate-service':
92	    case 'missing-service':
93	    case 'init-failed':
94	    case 'cycle':
95	    case 'timeout':
96	    case 'subscriber-failed':
97	    case 'unknown':
98	    default:
99	      // All other PluginError codes bucket to 500 INTERNAL. The specifics
100	      // (hookName, plugin, cause) are logged via logInternalError below; we
101	      // never paint them onto the wire.
102	      return { status: 500, body: { error: { code: 'INTERNAL', message: 'internal server error' } } };
103	  }
104	}
105	
106	/** Shape for "generic thrown error" fallback — 500 with opaque message. */
107	export function internalError(): HandlerErr {
108	  return {
109	    status: 500,
110	    body: { error: { code: 'INTERNAL', message: 'internal server error' } },
111	  };
112	}
113	
114	/**
115	 * Log an internal failure with full detail. Callers pass their request-scoped
116	 * logger so reqId is stamped automatically. We log at `error` level because
117	 * a 500 reaching the wire means something genuinely wrong happened — we want
118	 * it surfaced in production log pipelines.
119	 */
120	export function logInternalError(
121	  logger: { error: (msg: string, bindings?: Record<string, unknown>) => void },
122	  action: string,
123	  err: unknown,
124	): void {
125	  const base: Record<string, unknown> = { action };
126	  if (err instanceof PluginError) {
127	    base.pluginErrorCode = err.code;
128	    base.plugin = err.plugin;
129	    if (err.hookName !== undefined) base.hookName = err.hookName;
130	    base.err = err;
131	  } else if (err instanceof Error) {
132	    base.err = err;
133	  } else {
134	    base.err = new Error(String(err));
135	  }
136	  logger.error('ipc_dispatch_internal_error', base);
137	}
138	
139	// Internal re-exports so handlers don't need to know which file the type
140	// lives in. `IpcErrorCode` stays authoritative from @ax/ipc-protocol.
141	export type { IpcErrorCode };

Now let me think about the security implications and the design.

## Three threat models walk for this change

### 1. Sandbox escape / capability leakage
- New IPC handler composition: `session.get-config` IPC handler now ALSO calls `conversations:get-metadata` bus hook.
- Capability on the wire: a new field `runnerSessionId: string | null` in the IPC response. The runner already knows its own conversationId; runnerSessionId is opaque (matches existing wire field name in `conversation.fetch-history`). No new path-as-token confusion.
- ACL: The `conversations:get-metadata` hook performs `(conversationId, userId)` pre-filter. The userId on input comes from the prior `session:get-config` response — bound to ctx.sessionId via the bearer token. Runner cannot smuggle a different userId; the IPC handler controls the userId argument from the trusted bus response, NOT from the runner's request body.
- Edge case: if conversationId is a string from the session row but the conversation row was deleted (race), `conversations:get-metadata` throws `not-found`. We treat this as `runnerSessionId: null` and continue — safer than failing the entire session boot. No information leak (the runner already knows conversationId from the same response).

### 2. Prompt injection / untrusted content
- `runnerSessionId` is generated by the SDK (model-adjacent code) and stored opaque. The runner uses it as `query({ resume })` argument — the SDK validates internally. We never interpolate into shell, file paths, or HTML at any IPC layer. The host's `conversations:store-runner-session` handler has `z.string().min(1).max(256)` validation already in place; the wire shape it goes back out is the same string.
- N/A on prompt injection — we're not adding any new untrusted-string flow paths; we're just routing an existing opaque token through a different IPC.

### 3. Supply chain
- N/A: no `package.json` changes, no new dependencies. ipc-core already has session-inmemory + test-harness as dev deps; no new ones added.

OK, I'm clear on the design. Let me now implement.

// session.get-config
//
// Runner → host RPC fetched at boot. Authentication is the bearer token
// the runner already holds (set in its env by sandbox:open-session); the
// host's IPC server resolves the token to a sessionId and stamps that on
// ctx.sessionId before dispatch — this action's request body is therefore
// EMPTY by design. A non-empty body would mean the runner could ask for
// SOMEONE ELSE'S config; closing that door at the schema layer makes the
// invariant load-bearing.
//
// The response carries the FROZEN agent config snapshot (per Invariant
// I10 — switching agents = new session, not mutate). systemPrompt is
// USER-AUTHORED and intended to flow into the LLM's prompt; the runner
// must NOT interpolate it into shell commands, file paths, or HTML. We
// brand the field at the consumer side rather than here so subscriber
// hooks downstream of the runner don't need a wire-level brand.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export const SessionGetConfigRequestSchema = z.object({}).strict();
export type SessionGetConfigRequest = z.infer<typeof SessionGetConfigRequestSchema>;

export const AgentConfigSchema = z.object({
  systemPrompt: z.string(),
  allowedTools: z.array(z.string()),
  mcpConfigIds: z.array(z.string()),
  model: z.string(),
});
export type AgentConfig = z.infer<typeof AgentConfigSchema>;

export const SessionGetConfigResponseSchema = z.object({
  userId: z.string(),
  agentId: z.string(),
  agentConfig: AgentConfigSchema,
  /**
   * Conversation this session is bound to, when one exists. Nullable
   * because not every session is conversation-scoped (canary acceptance
   * tests, ephemeral admin probes, pre-Task-15 sessions). The runner uses
   * this to decide whether to call `conversation.fetch-history` at boot.
   *
   * The host populates this from the session row (Task 15 added a
   * `conversation_id` column to the session backend; the orchestrator
   * sets it at session-creation time). NEVER `undefined` — explicit null
   * keeps the wire shape stable across the schema bump (an absent field
   * would force every consumer to branch on three states).
   */
  conversationId: z.string().nullable(),
});

// invariant load-bearing.
//
// The response carries the FROZEN agent config snapshot (per Invariant
// I10 — switching agents = new session, not mutate). systemPrompt is

// must NOT interpolate it into shell commands, file paths, or HTML. We

//

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

// session.get-config
//
// Runner → host RPC fetched at boot. Authentication is the bearer token
// the runner already holds (set in its env by sandbox:open-session); the
// host's IPC server resolves the token to a sessionId and stamps that on
// ctx.sessionId before dispatch — this action's request body is therefore
// EMPTY by design. A non-empty body would mean the runner could ask for
// SOMEONE ELSE'S config; closing that door at the schema layer makes the
// invariant load-bearing.
//
// The response carries the FROZEN agent config snapshot (per Invariant
// I10 — switching agents = new session, not mutate). systemPrompt is
// USER-AUTHORED and intended to flow into the LLM's prompt; the runner
// must NOT interpolate it into shell commands, file paths, or HTML. We
// brand the field at the consumer side rather than here so subscriber
// hooks downstream of the runner don't need a wire-level brand.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export const SessionGetConfigRequestSchema = z.object({}).strict();
export type SessionGetConfigRequest = z.infer<typeof SessionGetConfigRequestSchema>;

export const AgentConfigSchema = z.object({
  systemPrompt: z.string(),
  allowedTools: z.array(z.string()),
  mcpConfigIds: z.array(z.string()),
  model: z.string(),
});
export type AgentConfig = z.infer<typeof AgentConfigSchema>;

export const SessionGetConfigResponseSchema = z.object({
  userId: z.string(),
  agentId: z.string(),
  agentConfig: AgentConfigSchema,

   * keeps the wire shape stable across the schema bump (an absent field
   * would force every consumer to branch on three states).
   */
  conversationId: z.string().nullable(),
});

// must NOT interpolate it into shell commands, file paths, or HTML. We
// brand the field at the consumer side rather than here so subscriber
// hooks downstream of the runner don't need a wire-level brand.
//
// Phase E (2026-05-09): the response also carries `runnerSessionId`. The
// Phase C deviation note on `ConversationFetchHistoryResponseSchema`
// argued the bind state should NOT ride session.get-config because the
// session backend doesn't own conversation rows. Phase E moots that —
// `conversation.fetch-history` is being deleted, and the IPC handler
// composes from two bus hooks (`session:get-config` + `conversations:
// get-metadata`) to assemble the wire response. The bus-level
// `session:get-config` output type stays unchanged; only the wire
// surface picks up the field.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export const SessionGetConfigRequestSchema = z.object({}).strict();
export type SessionGetConfigRequest = z.infer<typeof SessionGetConfigRequestSchema>;

export const AgentConfigSchema = z.object({
  systemPrompt: z.string(),
  allowedTools: z.array(z.string()),
  mcpConfigIds: z.array(z.string()),
  model: z.string(),

export type AgentConfig = z.infer<typeof AgentConfigSchema>;

   * keeps the wire shape stable across the schema bump (an absent field

  conversationId: z.string().nullable(),
  /**
   * Phase E (2026-05-09): the bound runner-side session id, or null if

   * `resume(sessionId)` vs a fresh SDK session. Wire name is camelCase,
   * opaque, and does NOT leak the specific runner shape (no
   * `sdk_session_id`, no `jsonl_path`).
   *
   * NEVER `undefined` — explicit null keeps the wire shape stable and
   * forces consumers to branch on three states (string, null,

   */
  runnerSessionId: z.string().nullable(),

});","new_string":"describe('session.get-config', () => {
  const baseConfig = {
    systemPrompt: 'be helpful',
    allowedTools: ['file.read'],
    mcpConfigIds: [],
    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-7',
  };

  it('accepts a response with conversationId set', () => {
    const parsed = SessionGetConfigResponseSchema.parse({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: baseConfig,
      conversationId: 'cnv_abc',
      runnerSessionId: null,
    });
    expect(parsed.conversationId).toBe('cnv_abc');
  });

  it('accepts a response with conversationId null (non-conversation session)', () => {
    const parsed = SessionGetConfigResponseSchema.parse({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: baseConfig,
      conversationId: null,
      runnerSessionId: null,
    });
    expect(parsed.conversationId).toBeNull();
  });

  it('rejects a response missing conversationId (must be explicit null)', () => {
    const r = SessionGetConfigResponseSchema.safeParse({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: baseConfig,
      runnerSessionId: null,
    });
    expect(r.success).toBe(false);
  });

  // Phase E (2026-05-09). runnerSessionId is the new field that lets the
  // runner skip a separate fetch-history round-trip — the bind state
  // rides session.get-config now that fetch-history is going away.
  it('accepts a response with runnerSessionId as a non-empty string (Phase C resume)', () => {
    const parsed = SessionGetConfigResponseSchema.parse({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: baseConfig,
      conversationId: 'cnv_abc',
      runnerSessionId: 'sdk-sess-resume',
    });
    expect(parsed.runnerSessionId).toBe('sdk-sess-resume');
  });

  it('accepts a response with runnerSessionId null (no bind yet OR no conversation)', () => {
    const parsed = SessionGetConfigResponseSchema.parse({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: baseConfig,
      conversationId: null,
      runnerSessionId: null,
    });
    expect(parsed.runnerSessionId).toBeNull();
  });

  it('rejects a response missing runnerSessionId (must be explicit null — same posture as conversationId)', () => {
    // Field is nullable, NOT optional. Forces consumers to branch on
    // three states (string, null, schema-fail) rather than treat absent
    // as "no opinion" — same wire-stability argument as conversationId.
    const r = SessionGetConfigResponseSchema.safeParse({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: baseConfig,
      conversationId: null,
    });
    expect(r.success).toBe(false);
  });

  it('rejects a response with runnerSessionId set to a non-string', () => {
    const r = SessionGetConfigResponseSchema.safeParse({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: baseConfig,
      conversationId: null,
      runnerSessionId: 42,
    });
    expect(r.success).toBe(false);
  });
});"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2494,"cache_read_input_tokens":102580,"output_tokens":1864,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2494},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1864,"cache_read_input_tokens":102580,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2494,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2494,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"diagnostics":null},"requestId":"req_011CaecsFnsadYoswsah1vgi","attributionAgent":"general-purpose","type":"assistant","uuid":"6b112260-e89f-4465-960b-eb791681285e","timestamp":"2026-05-02T23:35:43.350Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.worktrees/phase-e-runner-owned-sessions","sessionId":"7625cfa8-ece1-4865-b35c-bae1d71734d5","version":"2.1.126","gitBranch":"main"}

      ctx,
      parsed.data,
    );
  } catch (err) {

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// POST /session.get-config
//
// Runner-boot RPC. Calls the `session:get-config` service hook on the host
// bus; the session backend (postgres / inmemory) reads its OWN session row
// keyed by `ctx.sessionId` (set by the IPC server's auth gate after the
// bearer token resolves) and returns {userId, agentId, agentConfig,
// conversationId}.
//
// Phase E (2026-05-09): if `conversationId` is non-null the handler ALSO
// calls `conversations:get-metadata` to pull `runnerSessionId` (the bound
// SDK session id) and folds it into the wire response. The runner uses
// that to pick `query({ resume })` vs a fresh SDK session — it's the
// reader replacement for the now-deleted `conversation.fetch-history`
// IPC. The composition lives in the handler (not in the session backend
// hook) so the bus-level `session:get-config` output stays
// session-backend-only — conversations vocabulary doesn't leak across
// the bus boundary into a backend that has no business with it (I4).
//
// The userId we pass to `conversations:get-metadata` comes from the
// `session:get-config` response — bound to ctx.sessionId via the bearer
// token. The runner cannot smuggle a different userId; the handler
// controls the argument.
//
// `conversations:get-metadata` throwing `not-found` is treated as
// runnerSessionId=null + log + continue. It's a defensive path: in
// practice the conversation row exists if the session row references it,
// but a race (delete-during-boot) shouldn't kill the entire session
// boot. No information leak — the runner already learned conversationId
// from the same response.
//
// The request body is `.strict({})`. There is intentionally NO sessionId
// in the body — the runner cannot ask for someone else's config because
// the server reads ctx, not the body. A future change that adds a
// "sessionId" body parameter would need a strong reason and a security
// review; the schema keeps that conversation visible.
//
// Reject paths:
//   - PluginError(unknown-session) → 401 SESSION_INVALID
//   - PluginError(owner-missing)   → 401 SESSION_INVALID  (legacy session
//     pre-9.5 with no v2 row; the runner has no agent config to use)
//   - any other PluginError from session:get-config → mapPluginError
//   - PluginError(not-found) from get-metadata → log + null runnerSessionId
//   - any other error from get-metadata → mapPluginError / 500
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

// Bus-level shapes for `session:get-config` and `conversations:get-metadata`.
// We don't import the structural types from those plugins (no cross-plugin
// imports in handler code — the bus is the contract). The fields we actually
// read are pinned here.
interface BusSessionGetConfigOutput {
  userId: string;
  agentId: string;
  agentConfig: SessionGetConfigResponse['agentConfig'];
  conversationId: string | null;
}

interface BusGetMetadataInput {
  conversationId: string;
  userId: string;
}

interface BusGetMetadataOutput {
  runnerSessionId: string | null;
}

export const sessionGetConfigHandler: ActionHandler = async (rawPayload, ctx, bus) => {
  const parsed = SessionGetConfigRequestSchema.safeParse(rawPayload);
  if (!parsed.success) {
    return validationError(`session.get-config: ${parsed.error.message}`);
  }

  let cfg: BusSessionGetConfigOutput;
  try {
    cfg = await bus.call<SessionGetConfigRequest, BusSessionGetConfigOutput>(
      'session:get-config',
      ctx,
      parsed.data,
    );
  } catch (err) {
    logInternalError(ctx.logger, 'session.get-config', err);
    if (err instanceof PluginError) return mapPluginError(err);
    return internalError();
  }

  // Compose runnerSessionId from conversations:get-metadata when this
  // session is conversation-scoped. Non-conversation sessions
  // (conversationId === null) skip the metadata round-trip entirely.
  let runnerSessionId: string | null = null;
  if (cfg.conversationId !== null) {
    try {
      const md = await bus.call<BusGetMetadataInput, BusGetMetadataOutput>(
        'conversations:get-metadata',
        ctx,
        { conversationId: cfg.conversationId, userId: cfg.userId },
      );
      runnerSessionId = md.runnerSessionId;
    } catch (err) {
      // not-found: defensive log + null runnerSessionId. The session
      // row references a conversation that doesn't exist (delete race
      // or ACL drift). Continuing with a fresh SDK session is safer
      // than failing boot — no information leak (the runner already
      // knows conversationId from the response above).
      if (err instanceof PluginError && err.code === 'not-found') {
        logInternalError(ctx.logger, 'session.get-config', err);
        runnerSessionId = null;
      } else {
        logInternalError(ctx.logger, 'session.get-config', err);
        if (err instanceof PluginError) return mapPluginError(err);
        return internalError();
      }
    }
  }

  const composed: SessionGetConfigResponse = {
    userId: cfg.userId,
    agentId: cfg.agentId,
    agentConfig: cfg.agentConfig,
    conversationId: cfg.conversationId,
    runnerSessionId,
  };

  const checked = SessionGetConfigResponseSchema.safeParse(composed);
  if (!checked.success) {
    logInternalError(
      ctx.logger,
      'session.get-config',
      new Error(`response shape drift: ${checked.error.message}`),
    );
    return internalError();
  }
  return { status: 200, body: checked.data };

const OWNER = {
  userId: 'u-1',
  agentId: 'a-1',
  agentConfig: {
    systemPrompt: 'you are a poet',
    allowedTools: ['file.read'],
    mcpConfigIds: ['mcp-1'],
    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-7',
  },
};

interface Env {
  bus: HookBus;
  ctx: (sessionId: string) => AgentContext;
}

async function makeEnv(): Promise<Env> {
  const bus = new HookBus();
  await bootstrap({
    bus,
    plugins: [createSessionInmemoryPlugin()],
    config: {},
  });
  return {
    bus,
    ctx: (sessionId: string) =>
      makeAgentContext({ sessionId, agentId: 'ipc-server', userId: 'ipc-server' }),
  };
}

    await bus.call<SessionCreateInput, SessionCreateOutput>(

      ctx('init'),
      { sessionId: 's-cfg', workspaceRoot: '/tmp/ws', owner: OWNER },
    );
    const result = await sessionGetConfigHandler({}, ctx('s-cfg'), bus);
    expect(result.status).toBe(200);
    expect(result.body).toEqual({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: OWNER.agentConfig,
      // Task 15 (Week 10–12): conversationId is on the wire shape now,
      // null for non-conversation sessions like this one.
      conversationId: null,

    await bus.call<SessionCreateInput, SessionCreateOutput>(
      'session:create',
      ctx('init'),

        workspaceRoot: '/tmp/ws',

      },

    expect(result.status).toBe(200);

    const result = await sessionGetConfigHandler(
      { sessionId: 'someone-else' } as never,
      ctx('s-cfg'),

    expect(result.status).toBe(400);

import {
  HookBus,
  PluginError,
  bootstrap,
  makeAgentContext,
  type AgentContext,
} from '@ax/core';
import { createSessionInmemoryPlugin } from '@ax/session-inmemory';
import type {
  SessionCreateInput,
  SessionCreateOutput,
} from '@ax/session-inmemory';
import { sessionGetConfigHandler } from '../session-get-config.js';

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// session.get-config handler — direct unit tests
//
// Bypass the listener/dispatcher (no socket, no auth) — we drive the
// handler with a HookBus that has @ax/session-inmemory registered. The
// listener tests in @ax/ipc-server own the auth path; the handler tests
// own the shape contract: empty body in, frozen agentConfig out, the
// owner-missing / unknown-session error paths, AND the Phase E
// composition with `conversations:get-metadata` to fold runnerSessionId
// onto the wire.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

const OWNER = {
  userId: 'u-1',
  agentId: 'a-1',
  agentConfig: {
    systemPrompt: 'you are a poet',
    allowedTools: ['file.read'],
    mcpConfigIds: ['mcp-1'],
    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-7',
  },
};

interface Env {
  bus: HookBus;
  ctx: (sessionId: string) => AgentContext;
}

interface MetadataMock {
  /** runnerSessionId returned by the metadata stub when called with this conversationId. */
  byConversationId: Map<string, string | null>;
  /** Conversation ids whose metadata call should throw not-found. */
  notFound: Set<string>;
  /** Conversation ids whose metadata call should throw the supplied PluginError. */
  errors: Map<string, PluginError | Error>;
  /** Mutable call log so individual tests can introspect. */
  calls: Array<{ conversationId: string; userId: string }>;
}

function makeMetadataMock(): MetadataMock {
  return {
    byConversationId: new Map(),
    notFound: new Set(),
    errors: new Map(),
    calls: [],
  };
}

/**
 * Bootstrap the bus with session-inmemory and register a stub for
 * `conversations:get-metadata`. The IPC handler talks to BOTH hooks; we
 * stub conversations rather than pull in the real plugin so the tests stay
 * focused on the composition logic in the handler. The stub mirrors the
 * real plugin's ACL surface (throws PluginError(not-found) on a foreign or
 * unknown row).
 */
async function makeEnv(metadata?: MetadataMock): Promise<Env> {
  const bus = new HookBus();
  await bootstrap({
    bus,
    plugins: [createSessionInmemoryPlugin()],
    config: {},
  });
  if (metadata !== undefined) {
    bus.registerService<
      { conversationId: string; userId: string },
      { runnerSessionId: string | null }
    >(
      'conversations:get-metadata',
      'test-stub',
      async (_ctx, input) => {
        metadata.calls.push({
          conversationId: input.conversationId,
          userId: input.userId,
        });
        const errToThrow = metadata.errors.get(input.conversationId);
        if (errToThrow !== undefined) throw errToThrow;
        if (metadata.notFound.has(input.conversationId)) {
          throw new PluginError({
            code: 'not-found',
            plugin: 'test-stub',
            hookName: 'conversations:get-metadata',
            message: `conversation '${input.conversationId}' not found`,
          });
        }
        const rsid = metadata.byConversationId.get(input.conversationId) ?? null;
        return { runnerSessionId: rsid };
      },
    );
  }
  return {
    bus,
    ctx: (sessionId: string) =>
      makeAgentContext({ sessionId, agentId: 'ipc-server', userId: 'ipc-server' }),
  };
}

describe('session.get-config handler', () => {
  it('returns the frozen agent config for an owned session (no conversation → no metadata call, runnerSessionId=null)', async () => {
    const md = makeMetadataMock();
    const { bus, ctx } = await makeEnv(md);
    await bus.call<SessionCreateInput, SessionCreateOutput>(
      'session:create',
      ctx('init'),
      { sessionId: 's-cfg', workspaceRoot: '/tmp/ws', owner: OWNER },
    );
    const result = await sessionGetConfigHandler({}, ctx('s-cfg'), bus);
    expect(result.status).toBe(200);
    expect(result.body).toEqual({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: OWNER.agentConfig,
      // Task 15 (Week 10–12): conversationId is on the wire shape now,
      // null for non-conversation sessions like this one.
      conversationId: null,
      // Phase E (2026-05-09): null because conversationId is null —
      // metadata isn't called.
      runnerSessionId: null,
    });
    // Confirm the handler short-circuits the metadata call when there's
    // no conversation to look up — saves an unnecessary bus round-trip.
    expect(md.calls).toHaveLength(0);
  });

  it('returns conversationId when the session was created with one (Task 15)', async () => {
    const md = makeMetadataMock();
    md.byConversationId.set('cnv_test_1', null);
    const { bus, ctx } = await makeEnv(md);
    await bus.call<SessionCreateInput, SessionCreateOutput>(
      'session:create',
      ctx('init'),
      {
        sessionId: 's-conv',
        workspaceRoot: '/tmp/ws',
        owner: { ...OWNER, conversationId: 'cnv_test_1' },
      },
    );
    const result = await sessionGetConfigHandler({}, ctx('s-conv'), bus);
    expect(result.status).toBe(200);
    expect((result.body as { conversationId: string }).conversationId).toBe(
      'cnv_test_1',
    );
  });

  it('Phase E: composes runnerSessionId from conversations:get-metadata when conversationId is set', async () => {
    const md = makeMetadataMock();
    md.byConversationId.set('cnv_resume', 'sdk-sess-resume');
    const { bus, ctx } = await makeEnv(md);
    await bus.call<SessionCreateInput, SessionCreateOutput>(
      'session:create',
      ctx('init'),
      {
        sessionId: 's-resume',
        workspaceRoot: '/tmp/ws',
        owner: { ...OWNER, conversationId: 'cnv_resume' },
      },
    );
    const result = await sessionGetConfigHandler({}, ctx('s-resume'), bus);
    expect(result.status).toBe(200);
    expect(result.body).toEqual({
      userId: 'u-1',
      agentId: 'a-1',
      agentConfig: OWNER.agentConfig,
      conversationId: 'cnv_resume',
      runnerSessionId: 'sdk-sess-resume',
    });
    // Pin the input shape passed to conversations:get-metadata. The
    // userId MUST come from the session.get-config response (not from
    // any field the runner could control) — the test guards against a
    // refactor that wires userId from the request body.
    expect(md.calls).toEqual([{ conversationId: 'cnv_resume', userId: 'u-1' }]);
  });

  it('Phase E: composes runnerSessionId=null when get-metadata returns null (no bind yet)', async () => {
    const md = makeMetadataMock();
    md.byConversationId.set('cnv_fresh', null);
    const { bus, ctx } = await makeEnv(md);
    await bus.call<SessionCreateInput, SessionCreateOutput>(
      'session:create',
      ctx('init'),
      {

37	// Runner entry binary (claude-sdk variant).
38	//
39	// Spawned as a child process by a `sandbox:open-session` impl inside an
40	// isolated sandbox. Communicates back to the host over the URI in

44	// The runner holds NO LLM credentials (invariant I5). The vendored
45	// @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk calls api.anthropic.com through the
46	// host-side credential-proxy (see proxy-startup.ts); the SDK's outbound
47	// x-api-key carries an `ax-cred:<hex>` placeholder that the proxy
48	// substitutes for the real Anthropic key mid-flight. If the sandbox is
49	// compromised, the real key never entered this process.
50	//
51	// Shape: one persistent `query()` driven by an async generator that pulls
52	// user messages from the inbox long-poll. That keeps a single SDK session
53	// alive for the life of the runner instead of spawning a fresh one per
54	// chat turn — the SDK's internal conversation history carries across
55	// turns automatically.
56	//
57	// Exit codes (the spawning host branches on these):
58	//   0 — chat completed normally (inbox returned cancel; SDK drained).
59	//   1 — terminated abnormally (SDK threw, IPC errored after retries, etc.).
60	//   2 — fatal during bootstrap (missing env, initial tool.list failure).

69	
70	export async function main(): Promise<number> {

72	  try {
73	    env = readRunnerEnv();
74	  } catch (err) {

137	  // BEFORE the SDK query opens. Failure here is bootstrap-fatal — the

142	  // the host either crashed mid-bundle, or its workspace plugin returned

159	    );

161	    return 2;

164	  // Task 15 (Week 10–12): replay-at-boot. Fetch the persisted transcript
165	  // BEFORE constructing the SDK iterator so the model sees prior turns
166	  // before any live inbox message.
167	  //
168	  // Failure semantics: NON-FATAL. A storage hiccup or ACL drift here
169	  // shouldn't kill the chat — we'd rather hand the user a fresh
170	  // conversation than crash on resume. We log to stderr and continue
171	  // with an empty history.
172	  //

174	  //   1. `runnerSessionId !== null` — the conversation has a bound SDK
175	  //      session id from a prior runner. We pass it as `options.resume`

236	  const hostMcpServer = createHostMcpServer({ client, tools });
237	  const inbox = createInboxLoop({ client });
238	  // Phase 3: workspace commits are turn-end via git-status against
239	  // /permanent (`commitTurnAndBundle` at the SDK `result` boundary).
240	  // The legacy PostToolUse-based diff accumulator is gone — git status
241	  // catches ALL writes regardless of tool, including the Bash deletes
242	  // and MCP writes the legacy path missed.
243	
244	  // Tracks the last accepted workspace version so the host's optimistic-
245	  // concurrency check sees a coherent lineage across turns.
246	  let parentVersion: string | null = null;
247	
248	  // Phase C: bind the SDK's session_id to our conversation row.
249	  //
250	  // The Anthropic SDK owns durable transcripts on disk (under HOME, which
251	  // we redirect into the workspace in a sibling task). The first message
252	  // every `query()` emits is `{ type: 'system', subtype: 'init',
253	  // session_id, ... }` — see SDKSystemMessage in
254	  // @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk/sdk.d.ts:3282-3314. We capture that
255	  // session_id once and POST it to the host so a future runner restart
256	  // can `resume(sessionId)` instead of replaying the transcript from
257	  // our DB.
258	  //
259	  // Once-only: a single `query()` can re-emit system/init on a resume
260	  // path. Only the FIRST init is load-bearing for the bind — the runner
261	  // sets the flag BEFORE the await so a re-entrant init can't
262	  // double-fire even if the IPC is in flight.
263	  //
264	  // Non-fatal: if the bind fails, we lose the resume optimization on
265	  // next restart and fall back to fetch-history replay. The chat itself
266	  // continues uninterrupted.
267	  let runnerSessionIdSent = false;
268	  // Host-side bookkeeping for the final event.chat-end outcome. The SDK
269	  // maintains its OWN transcript internally; this array is only the shape
270	  // the host cares about (user/assistant text round-tripped through
271	  // AgentMessage). NOT the same as `replayTurns` above — replayTurns is
272	  // the persisted history we pull at boot to seed the SDK; chatEndHistory
273	  // is the within-process trace the host gets at chat:end.
274	  const chatEndHistory: AgentMessage[] = [];
275	
276	  // Per-turn content-block accumulators. Drained at the SDK `result`
277	  // boundary into event.turn-end so @ax/conversations can persist the
278	  // turn (Task 3 of Week 10–12). We track assistant and tool turns
279	  // separately because they emit as distinct chat:turn-end events:
280	  //   - assistant: text + thinking + tool_use blocks observed in
281	  //     `assistant` SDK messages within the current turn.
282	  //   - tool: tool_result blocks observed in `user` SDK messages whose
283	  //     content is the SDK echoing the tool-result back into the
284	  //     transcript. Replay (Task 15) needs these to reconstruct the
285	  //     conversation; the user-side text the human typed already
286	  //     reaches the conversation table via POST /api/chat/messages
287	  //     (Task 9), so we deliberately skip plain-text user blocks here.
288	  let turnContentBlocks: ContentBlock[] = [];
289	  let turnToolResultBlocks: ContentBlock[] = [];
290	
291	  // Most-recent host-minted reqId from the inbox (J9). Set when a user
292	  // message arrives; read by `event.stream-chunk` emissions during the
293	  // assistant branch below. Lifetime is "from the inbox pull until the
294	  // next inbox pull" — chunks for the SAME reqId may continue across
295	  // multiple SDK `result` boundaries (the SDK may break a long response
296	  // into multiple turns), so we DO NOT clear this on turn-end. A chunk
297	  // that would emit before any user message has been pulled is impossible
298	  // by SDK construction (no input → no output), but we defend anyway:
299	  // an unset reqId causes the chunk to be skipped (no `event.stream-chunk`
300	  // with a missing reqId — the host's router can't route it).
301	  let currentReqId: string | undefined;
302	
303	  // Inbox → SDK user-message generator. Closing via `return` on cancel
304	  // tells the SDK no more user messages are coming, which lets the outer
305	  // `for await (msg of queryIter)` drain naturally and exit.
306	  //
307	  // Task 15 (Week 10–12): replay-at-boot. We yield prior user turns
308	  // (role=user with text content) BEFORE pulling from the live inbox.
309	  // Assistant AND tool turns are skipped — the model regenerates the
310	  // tool flow from the user-side context. We can't re-yield tool turns
311	  // standalone: Anthropic's API rejects (400 invalid_request_error) any
312	  // tool_result block that isn't paired with a tool_use in the
313	  // immediately preceding assistant message, and we don't have the
314	  // assistant turn (since the prompt iterator only accepts user-shaped
315	  // messages). The conversation row still stores the tool turn (Task
316	  // 3's auto-append); on replay the SDK only sees user text turns and
317	  // the model regenerates tool flows from there.
318	  //
319	  // Phase C (2026-05-02): when `runnerSessionId !== null`, the boot
320	  // block above sets `replayTurns = []` so this generator only yields
321	  // the live inbox. The SDK rehydrates the transcript itself via
322	  // `options.resume = runnerSessionId` (set on the `query()` call
323	  // below). No double-replay.
324	  async function* userMessages(): AsyncGenerator<SDKUserMessage> {
325	    // ----- replay -----
326	    for (const turn of replayTurns) {
327	      if (turn.role === 'user') {
328	        // Collapse plain-text contentBlocks back into a string for the
329	        // SDK (matches the live-inbox shape so the model doesn't see a
330	        // mid-conversation format change). Multi-block user turns
331	        // (e.g. with images) yield the full block array.
332	        const allText = turn.contentBlocks.every(
333	          (b): b is TextBlock => b.type === 'text',
334	        );
335	        const content: SDKUserMessage['message']['content'] = allText
336	          ? turn.contentBlocks
337	              .map((b) => (b as TextBlock).text)
338	              .join('\n')
339	          : (turn.contentBlocks as unknown as SDKUserMessage['message']['content']);
340	        yield {
341	          type: 'user',
342	          parent_tool_use_id: null,
343	          message: { role: 'user', content },
344	        };
345	      } else {
346	        // assistant + tool turns are NOT re-yielded; the model
347	        // regenerates from the user-side context. Tool turns
348	        // specifically can't be yielded standalone (Anthropic 400:
349	        // tool_result without paired tool_use in the immediately
350	        // preceding assistant message).
351	        process.stderr.write(
352	          `runner: skipping replay of role=${turn.role} turn (model will regenerate from user-side context)\n`,
353	        );
354	      }
355	    }
356	    // ----- live inbox -----
357	    for (;;) {
358	      const entry = await inbox.next();
359	      if (entry.type === 'cancel') return;
360	      if (entry.payload === undefined) continue;
361	      // Capture the host-minted reqId so subsequent stream-chunk
362	      // emissions correlate back to the originating request. Both fields
363	      // are set on `user-message` entries by the InboxLoop layer.
364	      if (typeof entry.reqId === 'string' && entry.reqId.length > 0) {
365	        currentReqId = entry.reqId;
366	      }
367	      chatEndHistory.push({ role: 'user', content: entry.payload.content });
368	      yield {
369	        type: 'user',
370	        parent_tool_use_id: null,
371	        message: { role: 'user', content: entry.payload.content },
372	      };
373	    }
374	  }
375	
376	  let exitCode = 0;
377	  let terminatedReason: string | undefined;
378	  let terminatedError:
379	    | { name: string; message: string; stack?: string }
380	    | undefined;
381	
382	  try {
383	    const queryIter = query({
384	      prompt: userMessages(),
385	      options: {
386	        // Phase C: SDK resume(sessionId). When the conversation has a
387	        // bound runner session id, the SDK rehydrates the transcript
388	        // from its own on-disk store under HOME (workspaceRoot, see
389	        // below). Spread-conditional so the field is OMITTED on first
390	        // boot — the SDK's `resume?: string` typing is "string or
391	        // missing", not "string or null"; passing `undefined` would be
392	        // a type-level rather than a wire-level signal.
393	        ...(runnerSessionId !== null ? { resume: runnerSessionId } : {}),
394	        // ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is the `ax-cred:<hex>` placeholder (substituted
395	        // by the credential-proxy mid-flight); no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL — SDK
396	        // calls api.anthropic.com directly through HTTPS_PROXY.
397	        //
398	        // Phase C: HOME redirect for the SDK subprocess.
399	        //   - The k8s sandbox pod sets HOME=/nonexistent at the pod level
400	        //     so `git` (and any other tool the runner spawns) can't
401	        //     accidentally read a global ~/.gitconfig — git-paranoia.
402	        //   - The SDK needs HOME pointed at the workspace so its native
403	        //     ~/.claude/projects/<sessionId>.jsonl lands where the
404	        //     turn-end `git status + git add -A + bundle` captures it,
405	        //     closing the jsonl gap that workspace Phase 3 set up the
406	        //     plumbing for.
407	        //   - The runner-process git operations inherit HOME=/nonexistent
408	        //     from process.env (we don't override their env), so the
409	        //     redirect is targeted to this SDK subprocess only.
410	        //   - Side effect: the SDK's auxiliary files (`.claude.json`,
411	        //     `.claude/backups/`, etc.) also land in the workspace.
412	        //     Acceptable trade-off (Q1 of the Phase C plan): the `.ax/`
413	        //     filter in workspace:pre-apply doesn't subscribe validators
414	        //     to them, and we can split with a symlink/copy step in a
415	        //     follow-up if needed.
416	        //   - HOME is spread AFTER ...proxyStartup.anthropicEnv so this
417	        //     value wins on conflict. anthropicEnv currently doesn't set
418	        //     HOME, but defensive ordering matches the intent: we
419	        //     explicitly redirect HOME for the SDK subprocess.
420	        env: {
421	          ...proxyStartup.anthropicEnv,
422	          HOME: env.workspaceRoot,
423	        },
424	        cwd: env.workspaceRoot,
425	        disallowedTools: [...DISABLED_BUILTINS],
426	        // canUseTool stays as a belt-and-suspenders allow-path. The real
427	        // pre-call hook-bus forwarding happens in the PreToolUse hook below,
428	        // which ALWAYS fires (canUseTool only fires when the CLI decides a
429	        // tool needs a permission prompt — built-ins like Bash with benign
430	        // input don't reach it). See pre-tool-use.ts for the rationale.
431	        canUseTool: createCanUseTool({ client }),
432	        hooks: {
433	          PreToolUse: [{ hooks: [createPreToolUseHook({ client })] }],
434	          PostToolUse: [
435	            {
436	              hooks: [
437	                createPostToolUseHook({ client }),
438	              ],
439	            },
440	          ],
441	        },
442	        mcpServers: { [MCP_HOST_SERVER_NAME]: hostMcpServer },
443	        // Empty settingSources = SDK isolation mode: the runner does NOT
444	        // read ~/.claude, project settings, or CLAUDE.md. Config for this
445	        // sandbox arrives entirely through host-mediated IPC.
446	        settingSources: [],
447	        // Week 9.5: use the frozen agentConfig.systemPrompt the host wrote
448	        // at session-creation time. An empty string falls back to the SDK
449	        // preset (the dev-agents-stub seeds a default; production agents
450	        // require non-empty by validation). systemPrompt is USER-AUTHORED
451	        // and intended for the LLM — not interpolated into shell, paths,
452	        // or HTML.
453	        systemPrompt:
454	          agentConfig.systemPrompt.length > 0
455	            ? agentConfig.systemPrompt
456	            : { type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code' },
457	      },
458	    });
459	
460	    for await (const msg of queryIter) {
461	      if (
462	        msg.type === 'system' &&
463	        msg.subtype === 'init' &&
464	        !runnerSessionIdSent
465	      ) {
466	        // Set BEFORE the await so a re-entrant system/init (e.g. on a
467	        // future resume() path) can't double-fire while this IPC is in
468	        // flight.
469	        runnerSessionIdSent = true;
470	        if (conversationId !== null) {
471	          try {
472	            await client.call('conversation.store-runner-session', {
473	              conversationId,
474	              runnerSessionId: msg.session_id,
475	            });
476	          } catch (err) {
477	            // Non-fatal: we lose the resume optimization on the next
478	            // restart — the runner falls back to fetch-history replay.
479	            // The chat continues uninterrupted.
480	            process.stderr.write(
481	              `runner: conversation.store-runner-session failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}\n`,
482	            );
483	          }
484	        }
485	        continue;
486	      }
487	      if (msg.type === 'assistant') {
488	        const assistant: SDKAssistantMessage = msg;
489	        // Only plain text blocks round-trip into host history. Tool-use
490	        // blocks stay inside the SDK's session — the host observes tool
491	        // activity via event.tool-post-call, not via the transcript.
492	        const text = assistant.message.content
493	          .flatMap((block) => (block.type === 'text' ? [block.text] : []))
494	          .join('\n');
495	        if (text.length > 0) {
496	          chatEndHistory.push({ role: 'assistant', content: text });
497	        }
498	        // Accumulate full ContentBlock[] for the per-turn transcript that
499	        // ships to @ax/conversations via event.turn-end. Every block kind
500	        // ContentBlockSchema knows about is mapped explicitly:
501	        //   - text / thinking / redacted_thinking / tool_use
502	        //
503	        // Replay (Task 15) requires Anthropic-compatibility (J3): a
504	        // missing redacted_thinking block leaves a hole the model can
505	        // detect on a follow-up turn, so we MUST preserve it verbatim.
506	        // Unknown block kinds are dropped defensively so a future SDK
507	        // addition can't bypass the canonical schema.
508	        for (const block of assistant.message.content) {
509	          if (block.type === 'text') {
510	            turnContentBlocks.push({ type: 'text', text: block.text });
511	            // Per-block streaming (Task 6 / J9). The SDK delivers text
512	            // blocks as the model produces them; we forward each as a
513	            // `event.stream-chunk` so the host's chat:stream-chunk
514	            // subscriber (Task 5) can fan out to waiting clients (Task
515	            // 7). Empty-text blocks are skipped — emitting `{ text: '' }`
516	            // chunks is noise. Failure is non-fatal: the host may be
517	            // tearing down, and the canonical transcript still flows
518	            // via event.turn-end / event.chat-end. Untrusted (J2):
519	            // `block.text` is model output and reaches the host
520	            // verbatim — host-side renderers sanitize.
521	            if (currentReqId !== undefined && block.text.length > 0) {
522	              await client
523	                .event('event.stream-chunk', {
524	                  reqId: currentReqId,
525	                  text: block.text,
526	                  kind: 'text',
527	                })
528	                .catch(() => {
529	                  /* host may be tearing down; non-fatal */
530	                });
531	            }
532	          } else if (block.type === 'thinking') {
533	            turnContentBlocks.push({
534	              type: 'thinking',
535	              thinking: block.thinking,
536	              ...(typeof block.signature === 'string'
537	                ? { signature: block.signature }
538	                : {}),
539	            });
540	            // Same per-block streaming for thinking. The host's UI
541	            // toggles thinking visibility (Task 21 / J4), but the
542	            // chunk still travels with `kind: 'thinking'` so a
543	            // subscriber can route it to the right pane.
544	            if (currentReqId !== undefined && block.thinking.length > 0) {
545	              await client
546	                .event('event.stream-chunk', {
547	                  reqId: currentReqId,
548	                  text: block.thinking,
549	                  kind: 'thinking',
550	                })
551	                .catch(() => {
552	                  /* host may be tearing down; non-fatal */
553	                });
554	            }
555	          } else if (block.type === 'redacted_thinking') {
556	            // Redacted-thinking blocks have no human-readable text — the
557	            // model returned an opaque blob. We persist it (J3 — the
558	            // SDK detects holes on follow-up turns) but DO NOT emit a
559	            // stream chunk: there's nothing to render, and `kind`
560	            // wouldn't accept it anyway.
561	            turnContentBlocks.push({
562	              type: 'redacted_thinking',
563	              data: (block as { data: string }).data,
564	            });
565	          } else if (block.type === 'tool_use') {
566	            // Tool-use blocks are observed via event.tool-post-call
567	            // (when the tool actually runs) and persisted at turn-end.
568	            // They are not streamed text and have no `kind` mapping.
569	            turnContentBlocks.push({
570	              type: 'tool_use',
571	              id: block.id,
572	              name: block.name,
573	              input: (block.input ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>,
574	            });
575	          }
576	        }
577	      } else if (msg.type === 'user') {
578	        // The SDK echoes tool_result blocks back as `user` messages once
579	        // a tool finishes (the model issued a tool_use; the runner ran
580	        // the tool; the SDK threads the result into the transcript as a
581	        // user turn so the next assistant turn can see it). Replay
582	        // depends on these landing in the conversation row. Plain-text
583	        // user content is NOT collected: the human's typed message
584	        // arrives via POST /api/chat/messages (Task 9), and tool_result
585	        // blocks are the only thing the runner is the authoritative
586	        // source for here.
587	        const userMsg = msg as { message?: { content?: unknown } };
588	        const content = userMsg.message?.content;
589	        if (Array.isArray(content)) {
590	          for (const block of content as Array<{ type?: string }>) {
591	            if (block.type === 'tool_result') {
592	              const tr = block as {
593	                type: 'tool_result';
594	                tool_use_id?: string;
595	                content?: unknown;
596	                is_error?: boolean;
597	              };
598	              if (typeof tr.tool_use_id === 'string') {
599	                // Narrow array content to the text/image subset per
600	                // ToolResultBlockSchema (`string | (TextBlock |
601	                // ImageBlock)[]`). Image entries MUST round-trip — a
602	                // tool that returns image content (screenshot tool, Read
603	                // on a binary, etc.) loses context on replay otherwise.
604	                // Other entry types are dropped defensively so a future
605	                // SDK shape doesn't silently bypass the canonical schema.
606	                let normalizedContent: string | Array<TextBlock | ImageBlock> =
607	                  '';
608	                if (typeof tr.content === 'string') {
609	                  normalizedContent = tr.content;
610	                } else if (Array.isArray(tr.content)) {
611	                  const narrowed: Array<TextBlock | ImageBlock> = [];
612	                  for (const item of tr.content as Array<{
613	                    type?: string;
614	                    text?: unknown;
615	                    source?: unknown;
616	                  }>) {
617	                    if (item.type === 'text' && typeof item.text === 'string') {
618	                      narrowed.push({ type: 'text', text: item.text });
619	                    } else if (
620	                      item.type === 'image' &&
621	                      item.source !== undefined
622	                    ) {
623	                      // The SDK's image-block shape matches ImageBlock
624	                      // already; the .source discriminated-union is
625	                      // validated at the storage boundary by
626	                      // ContentBlockSchema, so no further narrowing here.
627	                      narrowed.push(item as unknown as ImageBlock);
628	                    }
629	                  }
630	                  normalizedContent = narrowed;
631	                }
632	                const normalized: ContentBlock = {
633	                  type: 'tool_result',
634	                  tool_use_id: tr.tool_use_id,
635	                  content: normalizedContent,
636	                  ...(typeof tr.is_error === 'boolean'
637	                    ? { is_error: tr.is_error }
638	                    : {}),
639	                };
640	                turnToolResultBlocks.push(normalized);
641	              }
642	            }
643	          }
644	        }
645	      } else if (msg.type === 'result') {
646	        // Turn boundary (Phase 3). Replaces the legacy PostToolUse-based
647	        // diff observer with `git status` + bundle:
648	        //   1. Stage everything in /permanent (`git add -A`) — catches
649	        //      ALL writes, regardless of which tool wrote (Bash, MCP,
650	        //      SDK Write/Edit/MultiEdit, raw fs, jsonl). Closes the
651	        //      Bash-delete + MCP-write + jsonl gaps that motivated
652	        //      the redesign.
653	        //   2. If nothing's staged → empty turn → skip commit-notify
654	        //      entirely (same heartbeat-only semantic the legacy path
655	        //      had for empty diffs).
656	        //   3. Otherwise: commit, build a thin `baseline..main` bundle,
657	        //      ship as `workspace.commit-notify`.
658	        //   4. On accept: advance refs/heads/baseline so the next turn
659	        //      bundles from the new state.
660	        //   5. On veto: roll the working tree back to baseline (the
661	        //      agent's writes for this turn are undone).
662	        //   6. On IPC error (host unreachable, 5xx): preserve the
663	        //      working tree as-is. Don't advance baseline; don't
664	        //      rollback. The next turn's `git add -A` will accumulate
665	        //      this turn's changes plus the next turn's, and we ship
666	        //      the combined bundle. Best-effort retry by accumulation.
667	        //
668	        // Failures here MUST NOT terminate the chat — `event.turn-end`
669	        // is still the heartbeat the host keys off.
670	        try {
671	          const bundleB64 = await commitTurnAndBundle({
672	            root: env.workspaceRoot,
673	            reason: 'turn',
674	          });
675	          if (bundleB64 !== null) {
676	            try {
677	              const resp = (await client.call('workspace.commit-notify', {
678	                parentVersion,
679	                reason: 'turn',
680	                bundleBytes: bundleB64,
681	              })) as WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponse;
682	              if (resp.accepted) {
683	                parentVersion = resp.version as unknown as string;
684	                await advanceBaseline(env.workspaceRoot);
685	              } else {
686	                // Host vetoed — surface to stderr (the host's log sink)
687	                // and rollback so the agent's next turn starts clean.
688	                process.stderr.write(
689	                  `runner: workspace rejected: ${resp.reason}\n`,
690	                );
691	                await rollbackToBaseline(env.workspaceRoot);
692	              }
693	            } catch (err) {
694	              // Network / 5xx / timeout: keep the working tree intact
695	              // so the next turn's accumulated changes flow as one
696	              // bundle. Don't advance baseline; don't rollback. Same
697	              // trade-off the legacy accumulator path made.
698	              process.stderr.write(
699	                `runner: commit-notify failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}\n`,
700	              );
701	            }
702	          }
703	        } catch (err) {
704	          // commitTurnAndBundle itself failed (git binary missing,
705	          // /permanent in a weird state, etc.). Non-fatal; the next
706	          // turn will retry.
707	          process.stderr.write(
708	            `runner: commitTurnAndBundle failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}\n`,
709	          );
710	        }
711	
712	        // One turn of assistant output finished. The SDK now awaits the
713	        // next yield from userMessages() — i.e. the next inbox pull.
714	        //
715	        // We may emit up to TWO chat:turn-end events at this boundary:
716	        //   1. role='tool' if the runner observed any tool_result blocks
717	        //      during this turn (the SDK echoed them back as user msgs).
718	        //      Emitted FIRST because they chronologically precede the
719	        //      assistant's wrap-up text in the transcript.
720	        //   2. role='assistant' for the assistant turn itself. Emitted
721	        //      unconditionally as a heartbeat — contentBlocks is only
722	        //      attached when non-empty so empty turns stay heartbeats.
723	        //
724	        // Failures here MUST NOT terminate the chat (host may be tearing
725	        // down). Each call swallows independently.
726	        if (turnToolResultBlocks.length > 0) {
727	          const toolBlocks = turnToolResultBlocks;
728	          turnToolResultBlocks = [];
729	          await client
730	            .event('event.turn-end', {
731	              reason: 'user-message-wait',
732	              role: 'tool',
733	              contentBlocks: toolBlocks,
734	            })
735	            .catch(() => {
736	              /* host may be tearing down; non-fatal */
737	            });
738	        }
739	
740	        const assistantBlocks = turnContentBlocks;
741	        turnContentBlocks = [];
742	        await client
743	          .event('event.turn-end', {
744	            reason: 'user-message-wait',
745	            role: 'assistant',
746	            ...(assistantBlocks.length > 0
747	              ? { contentBlocks: assistantBlocks }
748	              : {}),
749	          })
750	          .catch(() => {
751	            /* host may be tearing down; non-fatal */
752	          });
753	      }
754	      // system / partial / progress / etc. are SDK bookkeeping —
755	      // the host doesn't need to see them. (`user` messages ARE handled
756	      // above, but only to extract tool_result blocks for replay.)
757	    }
758	  } catch (err) {
759	    exitCode = 1;
760	    if (err instanceof Error) {
761	      terminatedReason = `${err.name}: ${err.message}`;
762	      terminatedError = {
763	        name: err.name,
764	        message: err.message,
765	        ...(err.stack !== undefined ? { stack: err.stack } : {}),
766	      };
767	    } else {

290	// conversation.fetch-history
291	//
292	// Runner → host RPC fetched at boot AFTER `session.get-config` returns a
293	// non-null conversationId. Returns the persisted turn-by-turn transcript
294	// so the runner can replay user-side history into the SDK's prompt

296	//
297	// Authz on the host: the bus-side `conversations:fetch-history` hook calls
298	// `conversations:get(conversationId, ctx.userId)` — that gate already
299	// enforces user ownership AND `agents:resolve`, so a runner that forges a
300	// foreign conversationId still can't read it. The runner authenticates
301	// IPC via its session bearer token; the IPC server resolves the token
302	// to ctx.userId, and that userId is what reaches `conversations:get`.
303	//
304	// Field-name conventions (I1):
305	//   - `turns` is generic. Each entry is `{ role, contentBlocks }` — same
306	//     shape Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini wrappers translate into. No
307	//     storage-backend vocabulary leaks.
308	//   - We deliberately do NOT include turnId / createdAt / turnIndex on
309	//     the wire shape. The runner replays into the SDK; ordering is
310	//     implicit in the array order, and identity is the LLM's concern,
311	//     not ours. A future audit/debug consumer that wants those fields
312	//     should call `conversations:get` directly (host-side hook).
313	//   - Phase C (2026-05-02): `runnerSessionId` is the bound runner-side
314	//     session id, or `null` if no runner has ever bound one. Runners
315	//     branch on `runnerSessionId !== null` to choose SDK
316	//     `resume(sessionId)` vs replay-from-DB. Wire name is camelCase,
317	//     opaque, never the snake_case `runner_session_id` from the DB row.
318	//     Trade-off: the host still loads `turns` from the DB even when the
319	//     runner is going to ignore them on a resume — the extra read is
320	//     cheap (one query at runner boot) and keeps the wire shape simple.
321	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

239	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
240	// session.get-config
241	//
242	// Runner → host RPC fetched at boot. Authentication is the bearer token
243	// the runner already holds (set in its env by sandbox:open-session); the
244	// host's IPC server resolves the token to a sessionId and stamps that on
245	// ctx.sessionId before dispatch — this action's request body is therefore
246	// EMPTY by design. A non-empty body would mean the runner could ask for
247	// SOMEONE ELSE'S config; closing that door at the schema layer makes the
248	// invariant load-bearing.
249	//
250	// The response carries the FROZEN agent config snapshot (per Invariant
251	// I10 — switching agents = new session, not mutate). systemPrompt is
252	// USER-AUTHORED and intended to flow into the LLM's prompt; the runner
253	// must NOT interpolate it into shell commands, file paths, or HTML. We
254	// brand the field at the consumer side rather than here so subscriber
255	// hooks downstream of the runner don't need a wire-level brand.
256	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
257	
258	export const SessionGetConfigRequestSchema = z.object({}).strict();
259	export type SessionGetConfigRequest = z.infer<typeof SessionGetConfigRequestSchema>;
260	
261	export const AgentConfigSchema = z.object({
262	  systemPrompt: z.string(),
263	  allowedTools: z.array(z.string()),
264	  mcpConfigIds: z.array(z.string()),
265	  model: z.string(),
266	});
267	export type AgentConfig = z.infer<typeof AgentConfigSchema>;
268	
269	export const SessionGetConfigResponseSchema = z.object({
270	  userId: z.string(),
271	  agentId: z.string(),
272	  agentConfig: AgentConfigSchema,
273	  /**
274	   * Conversation this session is bound to, when one exists. Nullable
275	   * because not every session is conversation-scoped (canary acceptance
276	   * tests, ephemeral admin probes, pre-Task-15 sessions). The runner uses
277	   * this to decide whether to call `conversation.fetch-history` at boot.
278	   *
279	   * The host populates this from the session row (Task 15 added a
280	   * `conversation_id` column to the session backend; the orchestrator
281	   * sets it at session-creation time). NEVER `undefined` — explicit null
282	   * keeps the wire shape stable across the schema bump (an absent field
283	   * would force every consumer to branch on three states).
284	   */
285	  conversationId: z.string().nullable(),
286	});
287	export type SessionGetConfigResponse = z.infer<typeof SessionGetConfigResponseSchema>;
288

31	## Open questions (need user decision before code)
32	
33	### Q1. The runner's `conversation.fetch-history` IPC: delete or stub?
34	
35	The runner uses `conversation.fetch-history` ONLY when `runnerSessionId === null` — i.e., pre-Phase-C conversations OR the very first turn of a fresh conversation (before `store-runner-session` has bound). After Phase D + Q3=(a):
36	
37	- Pre-Phase-C conversations: there are no production users; we accepted empty transcripts.
38	- First-turn-of-fresh-conversation: there ARE no prior turns to replay — the conversation just got created. fetch-history would return 0 turns regardless.
39	
40	Both paths hand the runner an empty `turns` array. Two options:
41	
42	- (a) **Delete the IPC entirely.** Runner unconditionally starts with `replayTurns = []`. Simpler. Removes the `conversation.fetch-history` schema, handler, dispatcher entry, and the `conversations:fetch-history` host bus hook in one PR.
43	- (b) **Keep the IPC as a stub returning `{ turns: [], runnerSessionId }`.** Lets the runner code stay structurally intact; only the host-side reader (the bus hook) goes away. Useful if we want to preserve a forward-compat hook for "give me the runner-bind state" without recomputing what `getConversation` already exposes.
44	
45	**Recommend (a).** The metadata read (`conversations:get-metadata`) already exposes `runnerSessionId`; that's the one signal the runner needs. The runner code already gracefully handles a missing fetch-history (`replayTurns = []` on error per `agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:209`). Replacing the call with `:get-metadata` (or skipping it entirely) is cleaner than maintaining a stub.
46	
47	### Q2. Migration to drop the table: SQL DROP TABLE or wait for a v2 migration?
48	
49	The table is `conversations_v1_turns`. Options:
50	
51	- (a) **Add a forward-only DROP migration.** One-liner: `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS conversations_v1_turns`. Idempotent (`IF EXISTS`). Greenfield; no production users.
52	- (b) **Leave the table on disk** (just unused). Cleanup later. Simpler now, harder to reason about later.
53	
54	**Recommend (a).** The table is dead weight; an explicit DROP makes the cleanup atomic and removes a footgun (a future plugin author seeing the table and assuming it's load-bearing).
55	
56	### Q3. Channel-web's user-turn append: how thoroughly to clean?
57	
58	`channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:309` calls `conversations:append-turn` to persist the user's POST'd message before dispatching `agent:invoke`. Phase D made this call dead-but-harmless (the row goes to a table no longer queried). Phase E removes the hook, so the call MUST go. The path also logs and unwinds errors; review whether anything is load-bearing besides the row insert.
59	
60	**Recommend:** delete the bus.call entirely. The user's message will live in the workspace jsonl as soon as the runner's first SDK turn writes it. The brief eventual-consistency window (between POST and the runner emitting the user line) is acceptable for MVP — channel-web's chat UI optimistically renders the user's typed message anyway from local state.
61	
62	If any other channel-web code path depends on conversation_turns being populated synchronously on POST, flag before code starts. (Quick grep: `grep -rn "conversation_turns\|conversations:append-turn" packages/channel-web/`.)
63	
64	### Q4. Test files: delete or refactor?
65	
66	These tests cover behavior we're removing:
67	- `packages/conversations/src/__tests__/fetch-history.test.ts` — entirely about a hook we're deleting.
68	- `packages/conversations/src/__tests__/store.test.ts` — half-and-half. The conversation-row CRUD tests stay; the appendTurn / listTurns tests go.
69	- `packages/ipc-core/src/handlers/__tests__/conversation-fetch-history.test.ts` — entirely about an IPC handler we're deleting.
70	- `packages/conversations/src/__tests__/subscribe.test.ts` — Phase D already removed the auto-append assertions; double-check no fetch-history-via-subscribe expectations remain.
71	
72	**Recommend:** delete the obsolete files entirely; surgically prune the half-and-half ones. Don't leave commented-out tests or `it.skip` zombies.
73	
74	If any of these recommendations are wrong, flag before coding starts.

Keep the wire-name camelCase, opaque (no `sdk_session_id`). Same wording style as the existing `conversationId` field's docstring. Note this supersedes the Phase C "NOT from session.get-config" decision — the rationale was wire-coupling between session config and conversation state, which Phase E moots since conversations no longer owns transcript replay.

After calling `session:get-config` bus hook, if `conversationId !== null`, ALSO call `conversations:get-metadata` with `{conversationId, userId}` (userId comes from the session:get-config response). Pull `runnerSessionId` off the metadata. Compose into the IPC response.

If `conversationId === null` → set `runnerSessionId: null` in the response.

If the `conversations:get-metadata` call throws PluginError(`not-found`) → log + treat `runnerSessionId` as null (defensive — should never happen, but prevents one bad row from breaking session boot). Any OTHER error from `conversations:get-metadata` → propagate as 500 (mapPluginError default).
